message: fix(ui): restore ftui hierarchy cues and score semantics (coding_agent_session_search-3tc1k)

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1jex8","title":"Phase 5D: Comprehensive keyboard shortcut system with ftui keybinding","description":"Replace the current hardcoded key matching (manual match on KeyCode variants scattered through the event loop) with ftui-core keybinding module. This provides: (1) Declarative keybinding registration with conflict detection, (2) Chord support (multi-key sequences like g+g for go-to-top), (3) Context-aware keybindings (different bindings active based on focused widget), (4) User-customizable keybindings via config file (~/.config/cass/keybindings.toml), (5) Keybinding display in help overlay auto-generated from registry. Define keybinding contexts: Global, SearchBar, ResultsList, DetailPane, Modal, CommandPalette. Support vim-like bindings (j/k/g/G//) alongside standard (arrows, PgUp/PgDn) with the ability to switch binding profiles. The ftui key_sequence module handles multi-byte sequences and timeout-based chord detection.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:23:10.490396895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:14.007441964Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:14.007228496Z","close_reason":"Merged into 2noh9.1.5 (Interaction model + keymap).  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1jl1w","title":"Phase 5C: Accessibility and degradation support","description":"Leverage ftui performance degradation system and accessibility features for inclusive design. ftui widgets check frame.buffer.degradation to adapt rendering at different performance levels: Full (all effects), SimpleBorders (ASCII borders), NoStyling (no colors), EssentialOnly (text only), Skeleton (placeholders). Implement: (1) High-contrast mode -- override theme with maximum-contrast colors for visually impaired users, togglable via command palette, (2) Screen-reader-friendly output -- widgets return is_essential()=true for content-bearing elements, false for decorative ones, (3) Reduced motion mode -- disable all spring animations and visual effects for users with motion sensitivity, (4) Large text mode -- scale up critical text elements, (5) Auto-detect terminal capabilities via ftui-core caps_probe (detect 256-color, truecolor, Unicode support) and gracefully degrade on limited terminals. Add an accessibility panel (inspired by the demo showcase a11y panel) showing current capability detection results.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:22:54.548434497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:13.709577857Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:13.709549565Z","close_reason":"Merged into 2noh9.6.3 (Final UI polish + a11y). Degradation levels, high-contrast mode, screen-reader, reduced motion, caps_probe merged.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1jl1w","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1cyg3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:34.847783482Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1jl1w","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1p0wb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:38.181974583Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1k44v","title":"Phase 1B: Implement CassApp::update() state machine","description":"Implement the update() method on CassApp that handles every CassMsg variant. This extracts all the key-event-handling logic from the current monolithic match block in tui.rs into clean, testable match arms. Each arm returns Cmd<CassMsg> for side effects. Key patterns: (1) QueryChanged triggers debounced search via Cmd::perform (async search then ResultsUpdated), (2) ResultsUpdated rebuilds panes via rebuild_panes_with_filter, (3) Selection/scroll changes update state directly with Cmd::none(), (4) Filter changes update filters struct and trigger re-search, (5) OpenInEditor spawns editor via Cmd::perform, (6) IndexProgress updates the progress display state. Critical: the update function must be unit-testable without a terminal -- pure state transitions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:16:49.298358931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:55:58.786463601Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:55:58.786434167Z","close_reason":"Merged into 2noh9.2.3 (Replace event loop). Pure update() state machine patterns (QueryChanged→debounce, ResultsUpdated, etc.) merged.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1k44v","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-34116","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:24:38.666320412Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1l0pn","title":"Fix results PageUp/PageDown to page selection deterministically","description":"## Goal
Make results-surface `PageUp`/`PageDown` navigation deterministic and user-visible by advancing selection page-wise while keeping viewport/scroll state coherent.

## Background
During post-close revalidation of the FrankenTUI results regression suite, we confirmed a semantics mismatch:
- `CassMsg::PageScrolled` currently manipulates virtualized scroll state, then synchronizes selection from stale state.
- In practice this can behave like a weak/no-op page action depending on viewport initialization and subsequent selection syncing.

Users expect page keys to provide clear navigation progress (selection + viewport movement), especially in long result lists.

## Scope
- Update results-focus handling for `CassMsg::PageScrolled` in `src/ui/app.rs` so page navigation:
  - advances/retreats selection by one viewport page (with clamped bounds),
  - keeps virtualized list offset consistent with selected row visibility,
  - remains deterministic before and after render cycles.
- Preserve existing detail-pane page scroll behavior (`FocusRegion::Detail`) unchanged.
- Keep `Home/End` semantics unchanged.

## Test Plan
Add/adjust unit tests in `src/ui/app.rs` covering:
1. PageDown from top advances selected row by at least one page when enough rows exist.
2. PageUp from a paged position retreats selected row and never underflows.
3. Repeated paging reaches terminal bounds deterministically.
4. Selection remains visible after render (no snap-back regressions).
5. Detail-pane page scroll path still adjusts `detail_scroll` and does not affect result selection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `PageUp`/`PageDown` provide obvious selection movement in long result sets.
- No regression in detail-pane scroll behavior.
- Existing results navigation tests continue to pass.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` all pass.

## Risks / Tradeoffs
- Changing page-key semantics may affect any tests or implicit assumptions that treated page keys as offset-only behavior.
- Mitigation: keep behavior explicit, covered by tests, and scoped to results-focus path only.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:27:52.183684749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:27:52.183684749Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","navigation","results"]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1l0pn","title":"Fix results PageUp/PageDown to page selection deterministically","description":"## Goal
Make results-surface `PageUp`/`PageDown` navigation deterministic and user-visible by advancing selection page-wise while keeping viewport/scroll state coherent.

## Background
During post-close revalidation of the FrankenTUI results regression suite, we confirmed a semantics mismatch:
- `CassMsg::PageScrolled` currently manipulates virtualized scroll state, then synchronizes selection from stale state.
- In practice this can behave like a weak/no-op page action depending on viewport initialization and subsequent selection syncing.

Users expect page keys to provide clear navigation progress (selection + viewport movement), especially in long result lists.

## Scope
- Update results-focus handling for `CassMsg::PageScrolled` in `src/ui/app.rs` so page navigation:
  - advances/retreats selection by one viewport page (with clamped bounds),
  - keeps virtualized list offset consistent with selected row visibility,
  - remains deterministic before and after render cycles.
- Preserve existing detail-pane page scroll behavior (`FocusRegion::Detail`) unchanged.
- Keep `Home/End` semantics unchanged.

## Test Plan
Add/adjust unit tests in `src/ui/app.rs` covering:
1. PageDown from top advances selected row by at least one page when enough rows exist.
2. PageUp from a paged position retreats selected row and never underflows.
3. Repeated paging reaches terminal bounds deterministically.
4. Selection remains visible after render (no snap-back regressions).
5. Detail-pane page scroll path still adjusts `detail_scroll` and does not affect result selection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `PageUp`/`PageDown` provide obvious selection movement in long result sets.
- No regression in detail-pane scroll behavior.
- Existing results navigation tests continue to pass.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` all pass.

## Risks / Tradeoffs
- Changing page-key semantics may affect any tests or implicit assumptions that treated page keys as offset-only behavior.
- Mitigation: keep behavior explicit, covered by tests, and scoped to results-focus path only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:27:52.183684749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:31:06.990519910Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:31:06.990491677Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic PageUp/PageDown selection paging + regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","navigation","results"],"comments":[{"id":488,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1l0pn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution evidence for `coding_agent_session_search-1l0pn` (2026-02-09)

Scope delivered
- Updated results `PageUp/PageDown` handling to move selection by viewport-sized steps instead of relying on offset-only page scrolling.
- Preserved detail-pane page scrolling behavior unchanged.
- Added deterministic tests for paging movement, bounds clamping, and detail-path isolation.

Touched surfaces
- Runtime behavior: `CassMsg::PageScrolled` in `src/ui/app.rs:9667`
  - Results focus path now computes `page_size = visible_count.max(1)`.
  - Selection advances/retreats by page-sized step with bound clamps.
  - Virtualized state is synced via `state.select(Some(target))`.
  - Detail path still uses `detail_scroll += delta * 20`.

- Regression tests: `src/ui/app.rs`
  - `results_navigation_page_scroll_and_home_end_are_stable` (`src/ui/app.rs:16360`)
  - `repeated_page_scroll_clamps_at_bounds` (`src/ui/app.rs:16400`)
  - `detail_page_scroll_does_not_mutate_result_selection` (`src/ui/app.rs:16425`)

Deterministic validation
- `cargo test --lib results_navigation_page_scroll_and_home_end_are_stable -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib repeated_page_scroll_clamps_at_bounds -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib detail_page_scroll_does_not_mutate_result_selection -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib results_surface_density_theme_matrix_preserves_core_cues -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib density_mode_switch_preserves_selection_and_scroll_state -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib results_focus_persists_through_theme_and_filter_changes -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `cargo check --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅

Tradeoffs
- Paging now intentionally prioritizes visible selection movement (orientation) over previous offset-centric semantics.
- Step size is driven by last rendered viewport visibility; fallback keeps progress deterministic when visibility is not initialized.

Residual risks / follow-up
- If product direction wants scroll-anchor-preserving paging (keeping same relative row line), a follow-up can refine offset strategy while keeping current selection guarantees.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:31:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lwxf","title":"Phase 4K: Clipboard integration via OSC52","description":"Leverage ftui-extras/clipboard for OSC52 clipboard integration that works over SSH and in any modern terminal. Add clipboard operations: (1) Copy selected result path+line to clipboard (Ctrl+Y or y), (2) Copy detail pane content to clipboard (Ctrl+C when in detail view without selection), (3) Copy code block from detail view (click or keybinding on specific code block), (4) Copy search query to clipboard, (5) Paste from clipboard into search bar (Ctrl+V -- currently handled by crossterm Paste event, but ftui clipboard provides a unified API). OSC52 is the escape sequence that allows terminal apps to read/write the system clipboard without any external dependency -- it works over SSH sessions which is critical for cass multi-machine search. The clipboard module handles terminal capability detection and graceful fallback.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:21:52.504734512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.027618302Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.027591773Z","close_reason":"Content preserved in new 2noh9.3 bead: Clipboard OSC52 integration.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3s2b","title":"Test Coverage Hardening Epic (No-Mock)","description":"## Epic: Test Coverage Hardening (No-Mock Policy Compliant)

This epic addresses test coverage gaps while strictly adhering to the no-mock policy. Focus is on:
1. **Unit tests for pure functions** - Parsing, validation, transformation logic
2. **Edge case coverage** - Error paths, boundary conditions, malformed inputs
3. **Fixture expansion** - Real data for untested scenarios
4. **Assertion quality** - Descriptive messages for test failures

### Current State
- Overall unit test coverage: <5% (intentional)
- Integration test coverage: ~85%
- E2E coverage: ~100% of critical paths
- Mock exceptions: 6 (in allowlist)

### Philosophy (from TESTING.md)
> Mocks hide bugs; real code paths matter more.

### Coverage Gaps Identified
| Module | Unit Tests | Integration | Gap Type |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------|
| Connectors (10+) | 0% | 100% | Edge case parsing |
| Pages/Wizard | ~20% | 80% | Path validation |
| FastEmbed | 0% | Integration | Model loading states |
| HTML Export | 0% | 95% | Encoding edge cases |
| Search Pipeline | <5% | 100% | Query parsing edge cases |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All connector parsers have edge case unit tests (malformed JSONL, truncated files)
- [ ] Pages path validation has unit tests (traversal, encoding)
- [ ] Query parser has comprehensive unit tests (special chars, unicode)
- [ ] Assertion messages include context for all test failures
- [ ] Coverage stays >=60% (current threshold)
- [ ] No new mocks introduced (or added to allowlist with justification)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:21:10.882590430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:57.558224565Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:57.558145719Z","close_reason":"All children completed: 819v (Pages Path Security), 6xnm (E2E Scripts), 27y8 (Connector Edge Cases), 1x2e (Assertion Quality), 335y (Query Parser Tests) - test coverage hardening complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3svt","title":"[Test] Pages export/encrypt/bundle pipeline E2E","description":"# Goal\nExercise the full pages export pipeline (export DB → bundle → encrypt → verify → deploy dry‑run) using real data and files.\n\n## Subtasks\n- [ ] Build fixture DB with mixed conversations/messages.\n- [ ] Run export to temp dir, then bundle/encrypt with recovery secrets.\n- [ ] Validate output file structure, manifest integrity, and decryptability.\n- [ ] Verify secret scan gating with real detection cases.\n\n## Acceptance\n- One end‑to‑end test that uses real files and keys, no mocks.\n- Detailed logging attached to test output.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:40:11.660597366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:24:34.371684645Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:24:34.371513657Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3svt","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-vh1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3t2r","title":"Task: Unit and integration tests for semantic search","description":"Comprehensive tests for semantic search in cass, including daemon client integration, embedding generation, reranking, and fallback handling.

## Test Categories

### 1. Daemon Client Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_daemon_health_check() {
    let client = DaemonClient::new();
    match client.health().await {
        Ok(info) => {
            assert!(info.uptime_secs >= 0);
            tracing::info!(uptime = info.uptime_secs, "Daemon healthy");
        }
        Err(e) => tracing::warn!(error = %e, "Daemon not running"),
    }
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_embed_via_daemon() {
    let client = spawn_test_daemon().await;
    let result = client.embed(&["test query"], Some("all-MiniLM-L6-v2"), None).await;
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    let embeddings = result.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(embeddings.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(embeddings[0].len(), 384);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_rerank_via_daemon() {
    let client = spawn_test_daemon().await;
    let docs = vec!["relevant doc about auth", "unrelated doc", "another auth doc"];
    let scores = client.rerank("authentication", &docs, Some("flashrank-nano")).await.unwrap();
    
    assert_eq!(scores.len(), 3);
    assert!(scores[0] > scores[1], "Relevant doc should score higher");
}
```

### 2. Semantic Search Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_semantic_search_returns_relevant_sessions() {
    let index = build_test_index_with_ground_truth();
    let queries = load_test_queries();
    
    for query in queries {
        let results = index.search(&query.text, SearchOpts::semantic()).unwrap();
        let relevant_found = results.iter().take(10)
            .filter(|r| query.relevant_ids.contains(&r.session_id))
            .count();
        
        tracing::info!(query = %query.text, found = relevant_found, "Search quality");
        assert!(relevant_found > 0);
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_hybrid_search_combines_bm25_and_semantic() {
    let index = build_test_index();
    let query = "authentication middleware";
    
    let bm25 = index.search(query, SearchOpts::bm25()).unwrap();
    let semantic = index.search(query, SearchOpts::semantic()).unwrap();
    let hybrid = index.search(query, SearchOpts::hybrid()).unwrap();
    
    // Hybrid should have elements from both
    let bm25_ids: HashSet<_> = bm25.iter().map(|r| &r.id).collect();
    let semantic_ids: HashSet<_> = semantic.iter().map(|r| &r.id).collect();
    let hybrid_ids: HashSet<_> = hybrid.iter().map(|r| &r.id).collect();
    
    let overlap = hybrid_ids.intersection(&bm25_ids).count() + 
                  hybrid_ids.intersection(&semantic_ids).count();
    assert!(overlap > 0, "Hybrid should blend BM25 and semantic results");
}
```

### 3. Fallback Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_search_works_without_daemon() {
    let engine = CassSearchEngine::new_direct(); // No daemon
    let results = engine.search("test query", SearchOpts::semantic()).await;
    assert!(results.is_ok());
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_graceful_fallback_on_daemon_crash() {
    let engine = CassSearchEngine::new_with_daemon();
    // First search warms daemon
    let _ = engine.search("warmup", SearchOpts::semantic()).await;
    
    // Kill daemon
    stop_daemon().await;
    
    // Search should still work via direct inference
    let results = engine.search("after crash", SearchOpts::semantic()).await;
    assert!(results.is_ok());
}
```

### 4. Reranking Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_reranking_improves_relevance() {
    let index = build_test_index_with_ground_truth();
    let queries = load_test_queries();
    
    let mut without_rerank_mrr = 0.0;
    let mut with_rerank_mrr = 0.0;
    
    for query in &queries {
        without_rerank_mrr += compute_mrr(&index, &query.text, false, &query.relevant);
        with_rerank_mrr += compute_mrr(&index, &query.text, true, &query.relevant);
    }
    
    let n = queries.len() as f64;
    tracing::info!(
        without = without_rerank_mrr / n,
        with = with_rerank_mrr / n,
        "Reranking impact"
    );
    
    assert!(with_rerank_mrr >= without_rerank_mrr * 0.95);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Daemon client tests (health, embed, rerank)
- [ ] Semantic search quality tests
- [ ] Hybrid search tests
- [ ] Fallback behavior tests
- [ ] Reranking improvement tests
- [ ] All tests pass with detailed logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:04:51.883142548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:02:35.286702287Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:02:35.286609164Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3t2r","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1b9z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:44Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tc1k","title":"Restore richer ratatui-era visual hierarchy in FrankenTUI results/detail surfaces","description":"Follow-up parity pass beyond initial mitigations: compare old ratatui and current ftui surfaces, recover high-signal visual hierarchy (agent color differentiation, snippet/readability density, accent contrast), and lock with snapshot evidence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:51:39.180419032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:51:39.180419032Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","parity","ui"]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tc1k","title":"Restore richer ratatui-era visual hierarchy in FrankenTUI results/detail surfaces","description":"Follow-up parity pass beyond initial mitigations: compare old ratatui and current ftui surfaces, recover high-signal visual hierarchy (agent color differentiation, snippet/readability density, accent contrast), and lock with snapshot evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:51:39.180419032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:56:39.053052402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:56:39.053028067Z","close_reason":"Completed visual hierarchy parity pass with score normalization + glyph stabilization + regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","parity","ui"],"comments":[{"id":489,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tc1k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed parity pass for results/detail visual hierarchy.

What changed:
- Normalized result-score visuals to a unified 0..10 scale (for both fractional 0..1 relevance and legacy 0..10 scores) so bar magnitude and high/mid/low tiers are meaningful again.
- Added explicit match-type cue () in non-compact result metadata for faster scanability.
- Increased compact snippet readability by using muted text weight for inline preview.
- Shifted detail metadata score display to include normalized context (e.g. ).
- Replaced emoji-heavy agent icons with deterministic single-glyph symbols to avoid fallback artifacts and width jitter in terminals.

Regression coverage added:

- score_normalization_and_bar_labels_support_fractional_relevance
- results_metadata_includes_match_type_cue_for_scanning
- agent_icons_are_single_char_glyphs

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tl5t","title":"Unpin install scripts from v0.1.64 and update version to current","description":"## What

The install scripts (install.sh and install.ps1) and README are pinned to v0.1.64, the last ratatui build. The README explicitly states: "Release gate note: install scripts are currently pinned to v0.1.64 (last ratatui build) unless you explicitly pass --version/-Version." Once the visual polish work is complete, this pin must be removed and the version bumped to the current ftui-based release.

## Where

- **install.sh** — Contains version pin (likely a `VERSION=` variable or hardcoded in download URL)
- **install.ps1** — Same pin for Windows PowerShell installer
- **README.md:17** — References `--version v0.1.64` in install command
- **README.md:25** — Release gate note explaining the pin

## Current State
```bash
# README.md line 17
curl -fsSL "..." | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify --version v0.1.64
```
```
# README.md line 25
Release gate note: install scripts are currently pinned to v0.1.64 (last ratatui build)
```

## Target
1. Determine the current version of the codebase (check Cargo.toml `version` field)
2. Update install.sh to use the new version (or remove the pin entirely if install scripts auto-detect latest)
3. Update install.ps1 similarly
4. Update README.md install commands to reference the new version
5. Remove or update the "Release gate note" — it should either be removed entirely or changed to note the new version

## Considerations
- This task should ONLY be done after ALL visual polish tasks are verified as complete. It is the final gate.
- The version in Cargo.toml may need to be bumped (e.g., from 0.1.x to 0.2.0 to mark the ftui milestone).
- Verify that the install scripts actually work with the new version before updating the README. Test on a clean system or in CI.
- The README install commands may also need the GitHub release tag to exist. Coordinate with the release process.
- Consider whether to create a GitHub release (tag + binary artifacts) as part of this task or leave that as a separate step.

## Acceptance
- install.sh no longer pinned to v0.1.64
- install.ps1 no longer pinned to v0.1.64
- README.md install commands reference current version
- Release gate note removed or updated
- Install scripts tested and working with new version
